record.title: Clara P. Grove, 1974-02-20, Interview #1 - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.occupation: Editor; cook; nurse's aid; columnist; leader of Women's Christian Temperance Union
record.origin: Born in Iowa, she lived in the Dakotas and Montana before coming in 1925
record.keywords: crops farming students boardinghouses University of Idaho log cabins immigrants women doctors suicide water downtowns police officers Prohibition WCTU local histories newspapers businesses death automobiles

point.title: Plowing alfalfa; alfalfa tea
point.synopsis: A different kind of alfalfa. Plowing alfalfa with a two bottom plow. Alfalfa tea.

point.title: Depression in Moscow; housing for students; students living in an abandoned cabin
point.synopsis: The depression in Moscow. Five dollars paid light, water, phone and groceries. Housing for students in the depression. Army camp in Moscow became property of U of I. Three students who lived in an abandoned cabin.

point.title: Foreign population in Moscow; Moscow businesses in 1920; women osteopaths; doctor who killed himself
point.synopsis: Self contained Moscow with no other wish. Foreign population in town. Kindness to the foreigners in the fifties. Business practices of 1920 Moscow. Women osteopaths in Moscow, their difficulty getting established. A man doctor who killed himself. Older families ran Moscow.

point.title: Water trough in Moscow; wooden sidewalks; police keeping peace during the Prohibition
point.synopsis: Water trough on Sixth Street in Moscow, wooden sidewalks. Quiet, peaceable town, places where men frequented kept quiet and undercover. Police kept peace in Moscow during Prohibition. WCTU during Prohibition

point.title: Settling Troy; her first income in Troy; working in the print shop
point.synopsis: How Troy was settled, two brothers planted rutabagas, that settled Troy. Olsen brothers built one house on boundary of first two homesteads in Troy. Mrs. Grove's first income in Troy came from working women for housekeeping. She and her daughter slept in the spare room until a man brought a cook car for them to live in. Worked half days in the print shop. House broken up because some women were through teaching.

point.title: Selling newspapers; fixing the printing press; blank spaces left for her to print on
point.synopsis: Wilbur Johns offers to sell newspaper (print shop). Got rid of paper just before her daughter graduated high school. Press said to be one that Mark Twain brought. Carl Olsen had to fix the press because it wouldn't turn (1930). Hand crank. Ever week a man had to carry the chase in. Ready print pages with two blanks for Mrs. Grove to print on. She eventually ran out of room on the blanks, needed more paper. [the], herself and outside correspondent "haunted news".

point.title: Parents keeping their daughter's body; treatment for sheep and horses
point.synopsis: Parents keeping daughter's body a week before burial. Treatment for bloated sheep and one for horses.

point.title: Newsmaking in Troy
point.synopsis: Newsmaking in Troy. "The case of the illegitimate baby."

point.title: Friendliness of Troy; men greeting her; special activities in Troy; car accident
point.synopsis: Friendliness of Troy, men lifting their hat as greeting to her. Special activities in Troy. Car accident on way to gather wild flowers for eighth grade graduation.

record.description: Editing Troy Weekly News. Local life in the twenties. 2-20-74 1 hr Emily Moore
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
